sections.paragraph:
καινότης, ητος, ἡ (since Thu. 3, 38, 5; Plut., Pericl. 13, 5; Lucian, Tyrannic. 22; 3 Km 8:53a; Ezk 47:12; Philo, Vi. Cont. 63) newness w. connotation of someth. extraordinary (καινός 2) of a star IEph 19:2. Hebraistically, the noun for an adj. κ. πνεύματος = πνεῦμα καινόν a new spirit Ro 7:6. κ. ζωῆς a new life 6:4; cp. IEph 19:3 (for lit. s. παλιγγενεσία 2). κ. ἐλπίδος a new hope IMg 9:1.—DELG s.v. καινός. TW.

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

someth. someth. = something

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

adj. adj. = adjective

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 497
